rationale: First make `page_array@.len() == NUM_PAGES` explicit so Seq indexing is well-typed, then collapse the two per-index ensures foralls into a single case-splitting `assert forall|i| ... post1@[i] == post2@[i]`. At `i == index` we re-assert each of the eight Page fields with `=~=` (matching the ensures syntax) for both runs and conclude `post1@[i] =~= post2@[i]`; at `i != index` we mention the trigger `post*.page_array@[i]` to fire the per-index ensures. Finally `Seq =~=` extensionality lifts the pointwise equality to `post1.page_array@ == post2.page_array@`, which is exactly what `det_set_state_equal` needs.
